title: Rise and dispute of Evergreen Analytics LLC
theme: Evergreen Analytics LLC, founded by Nadia P. Loe, incorporates and raises capital, and is then sued by its investors for misrepresentations in the offering.
narrative: Nadia P. Loe formed Evergreen Analytics LLC and elected S-corporation status. The company later raised money from Felix B. Fairbanks and Rosa B. Ellsworth, who now sue the company, its CEO Nadia P. Loe, and the outside auditor Northstar Builders LLC over alleged misrepresentations in the offering. The same company and founder appear as the applicant in the formation matter and as defendants in the litigation, tying the transactional and litigation matters into one story.

matter.title: Formation of Evergreen Analytics LLC and S-Corporation Election by Nadia P. Loe

jurisdiction.court_type: Maine Secretary of State / IRS (filing offices)

matter.summary: Nadia P. Loe is forming Evergreen Analytics LLC, a Maine corporation engaged in retail of specialty goods, and electing S-corporation status effective 2026-03-16; the engagement covers the Articles of Incorporation, the EIN application (Form SS-4), the timely S-election (Form 2553), and Maine corporate and payroll-withholding registration.

fact_pattern.summary: Nadia P. Loe is forming Evergreen Analytics LLC, a Maine corporation engaged in retail of specialty goods, and electing S-corporation status effective 2026-03-16; the engagement covers the Articles of Incorporation, the EIN application (Form SS-4), the timely S-election (Form 2553), and Maine corporate and payroll-withholding registration.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Nadia P. Loe is the founder and IRS responsible party for Evergreen Analytics LLC, a new corporation being organized in Maine to carry on retail of specialty goods. Articles of Incorporation were filed with the Maine Secretary of State with an incorporation date of 2026-03-16, and the corporation will have 3 shareholder(s) at formation.

The founder intends to elect S-corporation treatment under Subchapter S so that the corporation's income passes through to the shareholders, electing on a calendar year basis with a fiscal year end of December 31 and effective on 2026-03-16. The corporation expects 0 employee(s) and plans to begin payroll on or about 2026-01-18, using the accrual method of accounting and reporting under NAICS code 561730.

Because S-corporation owner-employees must be paid reasonable wages, the founder plans owner compensation of approximately $39970 per year and projects first-year revenue near $162577. The remaining work is to obtain an EIN on Form SS-4, file Form 2553 within the statutory window so the election is timely, register with Maine Revenue Services for corporate income tax and employer withholding, and set up payroll so the first Maine wage filings are correct.
undisputed_facts: Evergreen Analytics LLC is being organized as a corporation in Maine with an incorporation date of 2026-03-16.
Nadia P. Loe is the founder and the IRS responsible party for the corporation.
The corporation intends to elect S-corporation status effective 2026-03-16.
The corporation expects 3 shareholder(s) and approximately 0 employee(s) in its first year.
disputed_facts: Whether owner compensation of approximately $39970 satisfies the reasonable-compensation standard for an S-corporation owner-employee given the activity and revenue.
Whether the Form 2553 election will be filed within the statutory window so that S status is effective on 2026-03-16 without needing late-election relief.
Whether the accrual method of accounting is the appropriate choice for the corporation's first tax year.

timeline.event: Corporation begins payroll for its owner-employee and any staff, triggering Maine withholding obligations.
Articles of Incorporation for Evergreen Analytics LLC filed with the Maine Secretary of State.
EIN application (Form SS-4) and S-election (Form 2553) prepared for filing with the IRS for Evergreen Analytics LLC.

third_parties.name: Diego T. Ashford
third_parties.relevance: A second shareholder whose eligibility affects the S-corporation election and who must consent on Form 2553.

third_parties.name: Internal Revenue Service
third_parties.relevance: Issues the EIN on Form SS-4 and processes the Form 2553 S-corporation election.

exchanges.question: Tell me about the business and why you want a corporation with an S-election.
exchanges.answer: I'm starting Evergreen Analytics LLC to do retail of specialty goods. I want the liability protection of a corporation, but I'd rather have the profits pass through to me instead of being taxed at the corporate level, so an S-election makes sense.

exchanges.question: Who owns the company, and is everyone eligible to hold S-corporation stock?
exchanges.answer: There are 3 of us on the cap table. We're all individuals and U.S. persons, so I believe we all qualify, and everyone is on board with making the S-election.

exchanges.question: Will the company have employees, and what are your payroll plans?
exchanges.answer: We expect about 0 employee(s) to start, and I plan to put myself on payroll as an owner-employee. I'd like to run the first payroll around 2026-01-18, and I want to make sure we withhold Maine taxes correctly.

exchanges.question: Where do things stand on the EIN and the business bank account?
exchanges.answer: I still need the EIN before I can open the company's bank account, so getting Form SS-4 done is my first priority. Once the EIN comes through I'll open the operating account at the business bank.

exchanges.question: What does a successful setup look like for you?
exchanges.answer: Limited liability, pass-through taxation with a reasonable salary for myself, a clean EIN and payroll setup, and being fully registered in Maine. Above all, I cannot afford to miss the S-election deadline.

goals: Form Evergreen Analytics LLC as a Maine corporation with limited liability for its shareholders.
Make a timely and effective S-corporation election so income passes through to the shareholders.
Obtain the EIN and set up compliant federal and Maine payroll, including owner reasonable compensation.
Complete Maine registration for corporate income tax and employer withholding.
priorities: Filing Form 2553 within the statutory window so the S-election is effective on 2026-03-16.
Paying the owner-employee reasonable compensation that withstands IRS scrutiny.
Proper EIN, payroll, and Maine withholding setup from the first payroll.
desired_outcomes: An approved Maine corporation with an effective S-election as of 2026-03-16.
An issued EIN, a funded operating account, and correct first-year federal and Maine payroll filings.
non_starters: Blowing the S-election deadline and losing pass-through treatment for the first year.

constraints.budget: Limited; prefers to handle routine filings in-house with counsel reviewing the elections.
constraints.timeline: Needs the EIN and Form 2553 filed in time for the election to be effective on the incorporation date.

issues.issue: Maine corporate registration and employer withholding obligations
issues.governing_law: 13-C M.R.S. (formation); 36 M.R.S. § 5102 et seq. and § 5250 (income tax and withholding)
sub_questions: Are the Articles of Incorporation compliant with the Maine Business Corporation Act?
When must the corporation register for and begin Maine income-tax withholding on wages?
issues.client_position: The corporation will file compliant Articles and register with Maine Revenue Services for corporate income tax and employer withholding before the first payroll.

issues.issue: Making a timely and valid S-corporation election on Form 2553
sub_questions: Will Form 2553 be filed within 2 months and 15 days of the effective date?
Have all shareholders consented to the election?
issues.client_position: The founder intends to file Form 2553 within the statutory window with all shareholder consents so the election is effective on 2026-03-16.

issues.issue: Obtaining an Employer Identification Number on Form SS-4
sub_questions: Who is the responsible party for the EIN application?
What entity classification and principal activity should be reported?
issues.client_position: Nadia P. Loe is the responsible party and will apply for the EIN reporting the corporation's principal activity of retail of specialty goods.

expert_opinions.topic: Valuation support for owner compensation and initial capitalization
expert_opinions.opinion: The planned owner salary falls within a defensible range for the services rendered and the corporation's projected operations.
expert_opinions.basis: Market compensation data, the business plan, and the initial capitalization.

evidence.title: IRS Form 2553 S-election
evidence.description: The Election by a Small Business Corporation, with shareholder consents, electing S status effective 2026-03-16.

evidence.title: Shareholder agreement
evidence.description: The agreement among the shareholders covering ownership, transfer restrictions, and the S-election.

evidence.title: Initial capitalization / cap table
evidence.description: The cap table showing the 3 shareholder(s) and their initial capital contributions.

amounts.label: Expected first-year revenue
amounts.basis: Projected gross receipts for the corporation's first tax year.

amounts.label: Planned owner reasonable compensation
amounts.basis: Annual W-2 wages planned for the owner-employee.

amounts.label: Estimated first-year payroll
amounts.basis: Total projected wages for all employees in the first year.

amounts.label: Initial capital contributed
amounts.basis: Total cash and property contributed by the shareholders at formation.

related_matters.description: The company and its S-election set up the entity later sued by its investors.

matter.title: Fairbanks et al. v. Evergreen Analytics LLC et al.
matter.summary: Felix B. Fairbanks and Rosa B. Ellsworth invested in a purchase of LLC membership interests of Evergreen Analytics LLC and now sue the company, its CEO Nadia P. Loe, and (by third-party complaint) its outside auditor, alleging the offering misrepresented undisclosed contingent liabilities.

fact_pattern.summary: Felix B. Fairbanks and Rosa B. Ellsworth invested in a purchase of LLC membership interests of Evergreen Analytics LLC and now sue the company, its CEO Nadia P. Loe, and (by third-party complaint) its outside auditor, alleging the offering misrepresented undisclosed contingent liabilities.
fact_pattern.narrative:
On or about 2022-11-18, Felix B. Fairbanks and Rosa B. Ellsworth together committed roughly $3387890 to a purchase of LLC membership interests of Evergreen Analytics LLC, a closely-held Maine company led by its chief executive, Nadia P. Loe. The investment was solicited on the strength of an offering package and audited financial statements.

The plaintiffs allege that the offering materials materially misrepresented undisclosed contingent liabilities, that Nadia P. Loe knew or recklessly disregarded the true facts, and that the audited statements prepared by Northstar Builders LLC masked the problem. The plaintiffs say they discovered the misstatements around 2024-09-18, after which the investment lost most of its value.

The plaintiffs filed suit in Cumberland County Superior Court seeking damages of approximately $1921386. The company answered and counterclaimed, asserted cross-claims against its CEO, and filed a third-party complaint impleading Northstar Builders LLC, which issued the audited financial statements relied upon in the offering. The matter is now in active, contested discovery.
undisputed_facts: The plaintiffs invested in Evergreen Analytics LLC on or about 2022-11-18.
Northstar Builders LLC audited the financial statements used in the offering.
The investment's value declined materially after 2024-09-18.
disputed_facts: Whether the offering materials misrepresented undisclosed contingent liabilities.
Whether Nadia P. Loe acted with scienter or merely made an honest business judgment.
Whether the auditor's work fell below professional standards and caused the loss.
The amount of recoverable damages and whether punitive damages are available.

third_parties.name: Cobblestone Studios Corp.
third_parties.relevance: Marketed the securities to the plaintiffs.

third_parties.name: Casco Bay Savings
third_parties.relevance: Held the investment proceeds at closing; records trace the use of funds.

third_parties.name: Omar Q. Doe
third_parties.relevance: Covered the company and can speak to market expectations.

exchanges.question: How did you come to invest, and how much?
exchanges.answer: We put in about $3387890 into a purchase of LLC membership interests. We relied on their offering deck and the audited financials. Rosa and I went in together.

exchanges.question: When did you realize something was wrong?
exchanges.answer: Around 2024-09-18. The story they told about undisclosed contingent liabilities turned out to be false, and a former finance executive confirmed it.

exchanges.question: What is your view of the auditor's role?
exchanges.answer: Their name was on the financials we relied on. If they had done their job, this would have been caught.

exchanges.question: What outcome do you want?
exchanges.answer: Recover what we lost, hold Nadia accountable, and get there efficiently. We will go to trial if we must, but a sensible settlement is fine.

exchanges.question: How do you feel about the cost and exposure of litigation?
exchanges.answer: We know it is expensive and they are fighting hard with counterclaims. We are committed but cost-conscious.

goals: Recover the plaintiffs' investment losses, approximately $1921386.
Establish liability of the CEO for breach of fiduciary duty and fraud.
Preserve the third-party claim against the auditor as an additional source of recovery.
priorities: Maximizing leverage for a favorable settlement.
desired_outcomes: A judgment or settlement compensating the plaintiffs' losses plus costs.
A finding (or strong settlement posture) on fiduciary breach and misrepresentation.
non_starters: A walk-away that leaves the misrepresentation unaddressed.

constraints.budget: Funded through trial, but settlement is preferred if value is fair.
constraints.timeline: Wants the case trial-ready within roughly eighteen months of filing.

issues.issue: Whether the offering materials contained actionable misrepresentations
Whether the auditor is liable for negligent misrepresentation to non-client investors
The measure and availability of damages, including punitive damages
Whether the CEO breached fiduciary duties owed to investors

expert_opinions.topic: Tracing of proceeds and restatement analysis
expert_opinions.opinion: The audited statements overstated reported results for the relevant periods.
expert_opinions.basis: General ledger, bank records, and audit workpapers.

expert_opinions.topic: Compliance with generally accepted auditing standards
expert_opinions.opinion: The audit conformed to professional standards given the information available.
Plaintiffs' recoverable loss approximates the amount in controversy.

expert_opinions.topic: Application of the business judgment rule
expert_opinions.opinion: The CEO's decisions fell within a reasonable range of business judgment.

evidence.title: Email thread on revenue recognition
evidence.description: Internal emails discussing the accounting treatment.

evidence.title: Securities purchase agreement
evidence.description: The executed agreement governing the investment.

evidence.title: Board meeting minutes
evidence.description: Minutes approving the projections and offering.

evidence.title: Audited financial statements
evidence.description: The audited statements at the center of the dispute.

amounts.label: Estimated plaintiff-side fees and costs through trial

elements: Breach of the duty of loyalty or care
A false statement in the course of business

causes_of_action.claim: Violation of the Maine Unfair Trade Practices Act
elements: In the conduct of trade or commerce
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Actual damages, attorney fees, and costs

affirmative_defenses.basis: The complaint does not plead fraud with particularity. M.R. Civ. P. 9(b).
The challenged decisions were informed, good-faith business judgments.
The plaintiffs ratified the transaction by accepting later disclosures.
Claims accrued more than six years before filing. 14 M.R.S. § 752.

counterclaims.relief: Damages and injunctive relief for disclosure of confidential information.
Damages for allegedly false public statements about the company.

cross_claims.basis: If the company is liable, the CEO must indemnify it for his own misconduct.

third_party_claims.basis: The auditor's negligent audit caused or contributed to any loss.

discovery.description: First set of interrogatories on the offering and accounting.

motions.title: Motion in limine to exclude the damages expert
motions.relief: Exclusion of the plaintiffs' damages model.

motions.title: Motion to dismiss for failure to state a claim
motions.relief: Dismissal of the fraud and UTPA counts.

docket.entry: Complaint filed and served on the defendants.
Complaint and demand for jury trial filed.
Defendants served their Answer asserting affirmative defenses and a Counterclaim for breach of contract and tortious interference.
Defendants' answer, counterclaims, and cross-claim filed.
Third-party complaint against the auditor filed.
Defendants filed a Motion to Dismiss Count III (negligent misrepresentation) under M.R. Civ. P. 12(b)(6).
Scheduling order entered; discovery deadline set.
Client reports the defendants are liquidating the disputed inventory and moving sale proceeds offshore, threatening to defeat any judgment.
Order denying the motion to dismiss.
Order compelling production of the audit workpapers.
Plaintiffs' expert reports served; discovery continues.

procedure.event: Complaint filed and served on the defendants.
Defendants served their Answer asserting affirmative defenses and a Counterclaim for breach of contract and tortious interference.
procedure.deadline_rule: M.R. Civ. P. 7(a) / 12(a) — reply to a counterclaim within 21 days of service.

procedure.event: Defendants filed a Motion to Dismiss Count III (negligent misrepresentation) under M.R. Civ. P. 12(b)(6).
procedure.deadline_rule: M.R. Civ. P. 7(b)(1)(A) — opposition to a motion within 21 days.

procedure.event: Client reports the defendants are liquidating the disputed inventory and moving sale proceeds offshore, threatening to defeat any judgment.
procedure.deadline_rule: M.R. Civ. P. 65 — motion for a temporary restraining order / preliminary injunction; attachment under M.R. Civ. P. 4A/4B.
